cases.prompt: Set up pytest for this package
add coverage reporting with a fail-under gate to our test suite
can u set up test coverge so the build fails under 85%?
we need to run the tests on python 3.10 through 3.14
should we use tox or nox for multi-version testing?
add hypothesis property-based tests for the parser
my AI agent keeps rewriting tests until they pass - how do I make the suite catch that?
add mutation testing so tests that never assert anything get flagged
pytest can't find my tests since we moved to src layout
move the pytest config from setup.cfg into pyproject.toml
coverage comes back empty when I run pytest -n auto
our coverage number is high but regressions keep slipping through, what should we add?
pytest + coverage + tox setup pls
write unit tests for src/mypkg/utils.py
test_parse_headers is failing after my change, fix it
add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs pytest across a python version matrix
combine the coverage files from the CI matrix legs and upload them to Codecov in the workflow
set up pre-commit so the tests and linters run before every commit
switch the repo to src layout and fill in the pyproject.toml metadata so we can build a wheel
set up mypy in strict mode for this package
ruff is failing on the tests folder, fix the lint errors
delete the flaky test that keeps breaking the suite
bump the version and publish the package to PyPI
set up pytest with coverage for my python package
expected_behavior: checks the installed pytest version before choosing between [tool.pytest.ini_options] and the newer [tool.pytest] table
writes pytest config into pyproject.toml with testpaths and strict marker/config flags
enables branch coverage and sets a fail_under threshold in the 80-90 range, not 100
proves the coverage gate works by running pytest with an unreachable threshold and checking for a non-zero exit code
runs the test suite afterwards and reports the result

cases.prompt: we support python 3.11 through 3.14, make the tests run on all of them
expected_behavior: assesses project complexity before picking a runner instead of defaulting to tox
presents a plain uv run -p loop, tox, and nox as options with trade-offs
recommends a uv-backed environment backend when tox or nox is chosen
does not write GitHub Actions workflow YAML
verifies the suite runs on at least one non-default Python version locally

cases.prompt: add hypothesis property-based tests for our config parser
expected_behavior: targets properties like round-trip, idempotence, or invariants rather than re-asserting example outputs
uses bounded strategies instead of filtering generated inputs with assume()
registers separate dev and CI Hypothesis settings profiles
addresses persistence of the .hypothesis example database so discovered bugs stay found
runs the new property tests and reports failures honestly

cases.prompt: coverage is at 90% but bugs still keep shipping to users
expected_behavior: explains that coverage proves execution, not assertion quality
checks whether branch coverage is enabled and enables it if not
proposes mutation testing with a pinned tool and at least one alternative with trade-offs
suggests property-based tests for algorithmic or parsing code paths
does not respond by simply raising the coverage threshold

cases.prompt: an AI coding agent keeps making our tests pass by weakening assertions, harden the testing setup
expected_behavior: recommends mutation testing as the check on assertion quality
advises reviewing test-file diffs before production-code diffs in agent changes
warns that a green suite after the agent edited tests or conftest.py proves nothing by itself
hardens config so weakened gates fail loudly, for example a verified fail_under plus strict pytest flags
